Title: A chromosome-level Camptotheca acuminata genome assembly provides insights into the evolutionary origin of camptothecin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Camptothecin biosynthesis / monoterpene indole alkaloid (MIA) seco-iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of loganic acid toward secologanic acid/loganin branch point
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative methylation of loganic acid to loganin, but the C. acuminata enzyme failed to catalyze this reaction in vitro
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with/redirects flux away from the secologanic acid route; loss of activity enables direct conversion of loganic acid toward camptothecin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor chemotherapeutic compound; inhibits topoisomerase I
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low expression in all examined tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low expression in all examined tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; purified enzyme assay; UPLC; LC-ESI-MS/MS; protein structural modeling; mutagenesis of key residues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fails to produce loganin from loganic acid, supporting divergence of C. acuminata pathway toward secologanic acid and camptothecin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Only one candidate LAMT gene (CacGene05471) was identified in C. acuminata. 2. Recombinant CaLAMT failed to convert loganic acid to loganin, unlike CrLAMT. 3. Multiple C. acuminata-specific amino acid changes in the ligand-binding region reduced binding and enzyme activity, explaining functional divergence.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA80029); genome sequencing PacBio/Illumina (BioProject PRJNA639006)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of canonical LAMT activity eliminates loganin-forming branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Six activity-affecting mutations were inferred to be specific to C. acuminata; selection analysis suggested relaxed selection rather than positive selection for CaLAMT.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of loganic acid to loganin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates loganic acid to form loganin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Reference ortholog defining contrasting branch relative to camptothecin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor chemotherapeutic compound; inhibits topoisomerase I
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; purified enzyme assay; UPLC; LC-ESI-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Successfully converts loganic acid to loganin and serves as functional comparator showing CaLAMT loss of activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrLAMT catalyzed methylation of loganic acid to loganin in vitro. 2. It was used as the active reference enzyme contrasting with inactive CaLAMT. 3. Mutations corresponding to CaLAMT residues strongly reduced CrLAMT activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as the experimentally essential comparator from another species to define the causal metabolic divergence.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Camptothecin biosynthesis / monoterpene indole alkaloid (MIA) seco-iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Direct conversion of loganic acid to secologanic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: loganic acid; also loganin in prior report
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: secologanic acid; also secologanin from loganin in prior report
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative ring opening of loganic acid to secologanic acid; can also convert loganin to secologanin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor route toward strictosidinic acid and camptothecin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor chemotherapeutic compound; inhibits topoisomerase I
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in all tissues; higher than CYP72A610
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence alignment to previously characterized SLAS; phylogenetic analysis; tissue RNA-seq expression profiling; previous enzyme assay evidence cited; synteny/WGD analysis; positive selection analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes direct formation of secologanic acid from loganic acid, supporting the specialized camptothecin biosynthetic route in C. acuminata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CacGene10833 corresponds to previously characterized CYP72A565/SLAS. 2. It directly converts loganic acid to secologanic acid and can also convert loganin to secologanin. 3. It is highly expressed, has higher reported activity than CYP72A610, derives from the recent WGD, and shows positive-selection signals.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA80029); genome sequencing PacBio/Illumina (BioProject PRJNA639006)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in a syntenic block consistent with origin from recent whole-genome duplication; may also hydroxylate 7-deoxyloganic acid according to prior report.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Camptothecin biosynthesis / monoterpene indole alkaloid (MIA) seco-iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Direct conversion of loganic acid to secologanic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: loganic acid; also loganin in prior report
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: secologanic acid; also secologanin from loganin in prior report
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative ring opening of loganic acid to secologanic acid; can also convert loganin to secologanin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor route toward strictosidinic acid and camptothecin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antitumor chemotherapeutic compound; inhibits topoisomerase I
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in all tissues, but lower than CYP72A565
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence alignment to previously characterized SLAS; phylogenetic analysis; tissue RNA-seq expression profiling; previous enzyme assay evidence cited; synteny/WGD analysis; positive selection analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes direct formation of secologanic acid from loganic acid, complementing CYP72A565 in the specialized camptothecin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CacGene13172 corresponds to previously characterized CYP72A610/SLAS. 2. It directly converts loganic acid to secologanic acid and can also convert loganin to secologanin. 3. It is highly expressed, WGD-derived, and likely subfunctionalized relative to CYP72A565 due to lower expression/activity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA80029); genome sequencing PacBio/Illumina (BioProject PRJNA639006)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Positive selection signals were reported in SLAS genes; proposed to complement CYP72A565 for high overall conversion efficiency.
